methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $465M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing

data.narrative:
Photonic Inc is a Series E quantum computing company that has raised $375M CAD to date. The valuation universe comprises 10 comparable companies: 3 public peers (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum) and 7 private companies including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

Stage-progression analysis is applicable and anchors the valuation. Using total capital raised ($375M CAD) as a proxy baseline and applying a 1.4x step-up multiplier typical of Series E advancement yields an implied valuation of $651M. This method was selected because the peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables with overlapping valuation tiers to support a reliable peer-median approach. While 7 quantum computing private peers exist, none met the minimum threshold of three datapoints within filtered cohorts, making a narrow peer-median approach inadvisable.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $553M to $749M, with a median of $651M. This range reflects standard variance around the stage-progression result given single-method reliance. Key uncertainties include the absence of direct same-stage peer valuation data and reliance on total-raised as a proxy rather than explicit post-money marks. Validation would benefit from obtaining recent secondary transaction data or Series E-stage valuations from the identified private peer set.
